name: yale-law-journal-skills
description: An opinionated agent skill stack for legal scholarship aimed at The Yale Law Journal (YLJ), the student-edited flagship law review at Yale Law School. Twelve skills cover the law-review lifecycle: thesis and contribution, preemption checking, doctrine-to-normative argument structure, Bluebook footnote apparatus, multi-submission placement and expedite strategy, the student-editor review and source-pull, and the intensive edit cycle plus the online YLJ Forum.
